solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    bool canTransform(string start, string result) {
        int n = start.size();
        int i = 0, j = 0;
        while (i < n && j < n) {
            while (i < n && start[i] == 'X') ++i;
            while (j < n && result[j] == 'X') ++j;
            if (i < n && j < n) {
                if (start[i] != result[j]) return false;
                char c = start[i];
                if ((c == 'L' && i < j) || (c == 'R' && i > j)) return false;
                ++i;
                ++j;
            }
        }
        while (i < n && start[i] == 'X') ++i;
        while (j < n && result[j] == 'X') ++j;
        return i == n && j == n;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses two pointers to traverse both strings, skipping 'X' characters and comparing the order and relative positions of 'L' and 'R'. It ensures that 'L' moves left and 'R' moves right, which is valid per the allowed transformations. The approach is efficient and avoids unnecessary operations.
